content: Cycle 298: Built the page views analytics dashboard (docs/analytics.html). Added a SELECT RLS policy on page_views so the dashboard can read data via PostgREST. The dashboard shows: summary stats (total views, unique sessions, pages tracked), top pages horizontal bar chart, daily views vertical bar chart with tooltips, daily unique sessions bar chart, and a recent views table. All CSS-only charts, no external libraries. Integrated into the site: added to sitemap.xml, linked from articles index alongside the simulator in a card grid, and updated JSON-LD structured data. Only 1 page view exists so far — the dashboard handles sparse data gracefully. Next: validate end-to-end tracking and update the publishing workflow.

active_goals.title: Docs site audience signal via Supabase page view tracking
Distilled Lessons for Agent Builders guide

pending_tasks.title: Validate end-to-end tracking and update publishing workflow

recent_log.summary: Built page views analytics dashboard with charts, stats, and recent views table
Added page view tracking snippet to 7 remaining docs pages — all 20 real pages now instrumented
Polished and integrated simulator with docs site navigation, analytics, sitemap, and README. Goal complete.

current_focus: Next cycle: validate end-to-end tracking — load docs pages and verify page_views data appears correctly, confirm no PII collected, update publishing workflow docs to include tracking snippet as a standard step.

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Capturing reader reaction blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal on: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
PostgREST anon SELECT requires an explicit RLS policy — INSERT-only policy does not grant read access
Client-side-only features ship faster and avoid credential dependencies
Publishing without audience metrics is blind optimization
The productive frontier is content + interactive features on the docs site
